summary.intent: Implement GitHub issues #297/#298/#299 in the ars-ui Rust component library — the framework-agnostic core for 8 color components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker) plus a shared ColorValue foundation — in a single combined PR.
summary.outcome: All 8 components implemented and PR #698 created. 14 Codex review rounds addressed 60+ findings with TDD, specs kept in sync, and all xci-fast gates green. Pass 14 was being committed when the session ended (lock file removal, no_std float fixes, vertical gradient fix still pending final push/resolve/re-trigger).

repo: ars-core is no_std; transcendental float (powf, cbrt, atan2, rem_euclid) must use core_maths::CoreFloat in fully-qualified form CoreFloat::method(x), not inherent f64 methods
core_maths::CoreFloat lacks min/max; work around with private fmin/fmax helpers
MessageFn only provides From<F> impls for a fixed set of argument signatures; new color signatures need explicit From<F> impl blocks added to message_fn.rs
cargo xci-fast has a snapshot-count gate: each component with N state variants needs ≥N snapshots or the gate fails
CI uses `cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace` for coverage; cross-crate tests lift ars-components branch coverage ~1.1% above a -p-only measurement; branch threshold is 90%
spec validate runs on 340 files; spec-compile-snippets compiles spec code blocks in isolation — both are separate xci-fast gates
AngleSlider Part::Marker { value: f64 } cannot use #[derive(ComponentPart)] because f64 is not Eq/Hash; requires manual impl using to_bits() for hash/eq

Drag-machine pattern: core handles DragStart/DragMove/DragEnd transitions; adapter drives pointer capture; on_change_end fires via a typed Effect enum on DragEnd — use_platform_effects/track_pointer_drag do not exist
ars-components is no_std-capable; angle_slider's atan2/rem_euclid and color_wheel's rem_euclid are libm (std-only) and must be routed through CoreFloat
Controlled Bindable<T>: get() returns the stale controlled prop, pending() returns the internally-set value — display, ARIA, and hidden inputs must use pending() for consistency during drag/keyboard
ColorValue::new wraps hue into [0,360) — hue 360 becomes 0; linear sliders/area axes need a separate unwrapped tracked value (slider_value/x_value/y_value/hue_value) to reach the 360° endpoint
git add -A sweeps up .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock (written by ScheduleWakeup); always stage specific paths

code.finding: ColorValue::new must coerce non-finite components to 0.0 before wrap/clamp in release builds — debug_assert is insufficient
Use strip_prefix('#').unwrap_or(hex) not trim_start_matches('#') — trim_start_matches strips all leading hashes, accepting ##3366ff as valid
parse_color_string must use parse_finite_f64 (not raw parse::<f64>()) in all three parse_*_args functions; parse::<f64>() accepts NaN/inf strings
parse_hsb_args must clamp saturation/brightness to 0..=1 before HSB→HSL conversion; out-of-range inputs (200%) produce wrong colors otherwise
Hsb format must emit hsba() for translucent colors and parse the alpha channel — otherwise focus+blur on a translucent color silently sets alpha to 1.0

Four new From<F> impl blocks added for color signatures: Fn(&ColorNameParts,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,f64,&Locale), Fn(&str,&str,&Locale) — extend this list for any new message signature

ctx.invalid (external prop, owned by SetInvalid/SetProps/init) and ctx.parse_error (parser-derived, cleared by SyncValue/SetValue) must be separate fields; is_invalid() = invalid || parse_error — mixing them causes a prop refresh to drop aria-invalid

format_value shows 0–100 for percentage channels (saturation, lightness, brightness, alpha); commit_input must divide the input by 100 before clamping to 0–1; spinbutton aria-valuenow/min/max must also be scaled by 100
The IME is_composing early-return must check both ctx.is_composing and data.is_composing; a composing keydown before CompositionStart otherwise fires shortcuts through
apply_slider_position must invert normalized position (1.0 - pos) for horizontal RTL; track gradient uses 'to left' for RTL and 'to top' for vertical; render and drag position must be consistent
SyncValue re-derives slider_value from the incoming color; when the incoming color matches pending(), preserve the unwrapped tracked value to avoid the 360° hue endpoint snapping back to 0
apply_area_position must invert x (1.0 - x) for RTL; SyncValue must preserve x_value/y_value when the incoming color matches pending()
Blur must pass through the disabled/empty guard (add to always_allowed alongside SyncValue/SetProps); otherwise Focus→disable→Blur leaves machine stuck in Focused
columns:0 makes FocusUp/FocusDown no-op (modulo 0); clamp props.columns to max(1, columns) before row navigation
f64::clamp panics on min>max or NaN bounds; add a clamp_to_range helper that guards these cases for all clamp sites in the machine

workflow: Never use `git add -A` in agent sessions; always stage specific paths — the agent runtime writes .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock and other scratch files that get swept in
Delegate spec mirroring to a continuing SendMessage agent to protect main context; agent must run spec validate at the end and report
waiting-for-codex-review loop: post @codex review, poll for 👀, then poll until 👀 drops + threads appear or 👍 lands; reply+resolve each thread with fix name + test name, then re-trigger
Codex 'reached your usage limits' is a quota issue unrelated to code; stop the poll loop and wait for quota reset
Verify branch coverage locally with the exact CI command before pushing a coverage fix: `cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace` then `cargo xtask coverage check-all`
Stale Codex re-raises (line: null anchor, fix already in HEAD): reply citing the original fix commit + test name and resolve without re-implementing
In controlled-mode state machines, display/ARIA/hidden-input should consistently use pending() not get() for drag and keyboard interactions

friction: Codex rate-limited ('reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews') after the first trigger, blocking the loop until quota reset
.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock was committed unintentionally via git add -A starting at pass 5 and persisted for 9 commits before Codex flagged it
Zsh read-only `status` variable crashed two background CI watch scripts
Spec described non-existent APIs (use_platform_effects, track_pointer_drag) requiring full reconciliation across all 8 spec files
Coverage CI failed after the initial push (branch 89.7% < 90%) due to untested one-armed if-flag conditionals in *_attrs methods; fixing it also revealed the dead has_description branch
14 Codex review rounds (60+ findings) on agnostic core with no adapter consumer; later rounds were deep controlled-mode/RTL edge cases
core_maths::CoreFloat lacks min/max, requiring private fmin/fmax helpers despite the workspace's CoreFloat convention
TransitionPlan closure type inference (E0282) cascaded through all early-return closures, requiring explicit |ctx: &mut Context| annotations everywhere
Bash safety classifier temporarily unavailable during one push/reply cycle
open_items: Pass 14 was mid-commit when the session ended — needs push confirmed, 5 threads replied+resolved (S1 citing core_maths approval, S2 lock-file removal, S3 vertical gradient, S4 angle no_std, S5 wheel no_std), and @codex review re-triggered
PR #698 awaits merge decision after Codex 👍
Adapter implementations (Leptos/Dioxus color component wrappers) are explicitly out of scope for #297-299 and need separate issues
ColorValue shared types could be promoted from color-picker.md §Types to spec/shared/color-types.md (deferred to avoid touching manifest + 8 frontmatters)
spec/foundation/04-internationalization.md MessageFn impl listing is intentionally non-exhaustive and omits all color-component signatures — may cause confusion for future contributors
